title: HTTP Representations, Validators, Ranges, and File Transfer · Runtime Contract: Validators Conditionals
summary: A download endpoint guesses MIME types, buffers large bodies, ignores validators and Range, and treats Content-Disposition text as trusted HTML. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the validators conditionals increment in HTTP file-representation and transfer contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply ETag and Last-Modified validators with If-Match, If-None-Match, If-Range, 304, 412, and changed-state evidence.
Define media type, content length, content encoding, language, disposition, filename parameters, and cache metadata as representation facts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Validators Conditionals · Theory Validators Conditionals
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 8962 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate conditional inclusive byte ranges and response-body cleanup; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Apply ETag and Last-Modified validators with If-Match, If-None-Match, If-Range, 304, 412, and changed-state evidence.
